english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns’ rules

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The Nuns’ rules

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Preliminaries

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Preliminaries

segments.pali: Sammajjanī padīpo ca, udakaṁ āsanena ca;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sweeping and lamps, setting up of water and seats;

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Sweeping, lamps, setting up of water and seats;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: these are to be done before the observance-day ceremony.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: these are to be done before the observance-day ceremony.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Preliminary duties

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Preliminary Duties

segments.pali: Chanda, pārisuddhi, utukkhānaṁ, bhikkhunigaṇanā ca ovādo;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Consent, purity, knowing the season, and counting the nuns, instruction;

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Consent, purity, knowing the season, counting the nuns and Instruction;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: these are called the preliminary duties of the observance-day ceremony.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: these are the preliminary duties of the observance-day ceremony.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The factors of readiness

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The appropriate time

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It is the observance day, all the nuns who should be present have arrived,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The observance-day, all the nuns have arrived for the procedure;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are no shared offenses,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: none have shared offenses

segments.pali: Vajjanīyā ca puggalā tasmiṁ na honti, “pattakalla”nti vuccati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and there is no-one there who should not be present. This is called readiness.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and there are no persons to be avoided. This is called the appropriate time.

segments.pali: Pubbakaraṇapubbakiccāni samāpetvā desitāpattikassa samaggassa bhikkhunisaṅghassa anumatiyā pātimokkhaṁ uddisituṁ ārādhanaṁ karoma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Having completed the duties to be done beforehand and the preliminary duties, with the assent of the nuns’ community, one and all, with offenses confessed, I invite the recitation of the Monastic Code.”

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Having complete the duties to be done beforehand and the preliminary duties, with the assent of the Nuns’ community, one and all, with offences confessed, I invite the recitation of the Monastic Code.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The recitation of the introduction

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Recitation of the Introduction

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Please, Venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Today is the observance day, the fifteenth.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Today is the observance day, the fifteenth.

segments.pali: yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho uposathaṁ kareyya, pātimokkhaṁ uddiseyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should do the observance-day ceremony, it should recite the Monastic Code.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If it seems appropriate to the Sangha, it should do the observance-day ceremony, it should recite the Monastic Code.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: What is the preliminary duty of the Sangha?

english.author: pasanna
english.text: What is the preliminary duty of the Sangha?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The venerables should declare their purity.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The venerables should declare their purity.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I will recite the Monastic Code.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: I will recite the Monastic Code.

segments.pali: taṁ sabbāva santā sādhukaṁ suṇoma manasi karoma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Everyone present should listen to it and attend carefully.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Everyone present should listen to it and attend carefully.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Anyone who has committed an offense should reveal it.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Anyone who has committed an offense should reveal it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If you haven’t committed any offense, you should remain silent.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If you haven’t committed any offense, you should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If you are silent, I will regard you as pure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If you are silent, I will regard you as pure.

segments.pali: Yathā kho pana paccekapuṭṭhassā veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti, evamevaṁ evarūpāya parisāya yāvatatiyaṁ anusāvitaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just as one responds when asked individually, so too, an announcement is made three times in this kind of gathering.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Just as one responds when asked individually, so too, an announcement is made three times in this kind of gathering.

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ anusāviyamāne saramānā santiṁ āpattiṁ nāvikareyya, sampajānamusāvādassā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun remembers an offense while the announcement is being made up to the third time, but doesn’t reveal it, she is lying in full awareness.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun remembers an offense while the announcement is being made up to the third time, but doesn’t reveal it, she is lying in full awareness.

segments.pali: Sampajānamusāvādo kho panāyyāyo, antarāyiko dhammo vutto bhagavatā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lying in full awareness is called an obstacle by the Buddha.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Lying in full awareness is called an obstacle by the Buddha.

segments.pali: tasmā saramānāya bhikkhuniyā āpannāya visuddhāpekkhāya santī āpatti āvikātabbā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A nun who remembers an offense and is seeking purification should therefore reveal it.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A nun who remembers an offense and is seeking purification should therefore reveal it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it’s revealed, she will be at ease.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When it’s revealed, she will be at ease.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, the introduction has been recited.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, the introduction has been recited;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The introduction is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The introduction is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The recitation of the rules on expulsion

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Recitation of the Rules of Expulsion

segments.pali: Tatrime aṭṭha pārājikā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: These eight rules on expulsion come up for recitation.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, these eight rules on expulsion come up for recitation.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 1: sexual intercourse

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 1. Sexual Intercourse

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī chandaso methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseveyya, antamaso tiracchānagatenapi, pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun willingly has sexual intercourse, even with a male animal, she is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun willingly has sexual intercourse, even with a male animal, she is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 2: stealing

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 2. Theft

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī gāmā vā araññā vā adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyeyya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun, intending to steal, takes from an inhabited area or from the wilderness what has not been given to her—

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun, intending to steal, takes from an inhabited area or from the wilderness what has not been given to her—

segments.pali: yathārūpe adinnādāne rājāno coraṁ gahetvā haneyyuṁ vā bandheyyuṁ vā pabbājeyyuṁ vā corāsi bālāsi mūḷhāsi thenāsīti, tathārūpaṁ bhikkhunī adinnaṁ ādiyamānā

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the sort of stealing for which kings, having caught a thief, would beat, imprison, or banish her, saying, ‘You’re a bandit, you’re a fool, you’ve gone astray, you’re a thief’—

english.author: pasanna
english.text: the sort of stealing for which kings, having caught a thief, would beat, imprison, or banish her, saying, ‘You’re a bandit, you’re a fool, you’ve gone astray, you’re a thief’—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she too is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: she is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 3: killing a human being

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 3. Murder

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropeyya, satthahārakaṁ vāssa pariyeseyya, maraṇavaṇṇaṁ vā saṁvaṇṇeyya, maraṇāya vā samādapeyya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun intentionally kills a human being or seeks an instrument of death for them or praises death or incites someone to die, saying,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun intentionally kills a human being or seeks an instrument of death for her or praises death or incites someone to die, saying,

segments.pali: “ambho purisa, kiṁ tuyhiminā pāpakena dujjīvitena, mataṁ te jīvitā seyyo”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘My friend, what’s the point of this miserable and difficult life? Death is better for you than life!’—

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘My friend, what’s the point of this miserable and difficult life? Death is better for you than life!’—

segments.pali: iti cittamanā cittasaṅkappā anekapariyāyena maraṇavaṇṇaṁ vā saṁvaṇṇeyya, maraṇāya vā samādapeyya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: thinking and intending thus, if she praises death in many ways or incites someone to die—

english.author: pasanna
english.text: thinking and intending thus, if she praises death in many ways or incites someone to die—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she too is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: she too is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 4: falsely claiming a superhuman quality

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 3. Laying False Claim to Superhuman Attainments

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī anabhijānaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ attupanāyikaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanaṁ samudācareyya, “iti jānāmi, iti passāmī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun falsely claims for herself a superhuman quality, a knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, saying, ‘This I know, this I see,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun falsely claims for herself a superhuman quality, a knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, saying, ‘This I know, this I see,’

segments.pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamānā vā asamanuggāhīyamānā vā āpannā visuddhāpekkhā evaṁ vadeyya, “ajānamevaṁ, ayye, avacaṁ jānāmi, apassaṁ passāmi, tucchaṁ musā vilapi”nti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: but after some time—whether she is questioned or not, but having committed the offense and seeking purification—should say: ‘Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see; what I said was empty and false,’ then,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: but after some time—whether she is questioned or not, but having committed the offense and seeking purification—should say: ‘Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see; what I said was empty and false,’ then,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: except if it is due to overestimation,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: except if it is due to overestimation,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she too is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: she too is expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 5: physical contact with a man

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 5. Above the Knees

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa, adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ āmasanaṁ vā parāmasanaṁ vā gahaṇaṁ vā chupanaṁ vā paṭipīḷanaṁ vā sādiyeyya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a lustful nun consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, to touching her, to taking hold of her, to contacting her, or to squeezing her, anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on above the knees.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a lustful nun consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, to touching her, to taking hold of her, to contacting her, or to squeezing her, anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on above the knees.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 6: concealing an expulsion offense

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 6. Conceal Offenses

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodeyya, na gaṇassa āroceyya, yadā ca sā ṭhitā vā assa cutā vā nāsitā vā avassaṭā vā, sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya: “pubbevāhaṁ, ayye, aññāsiṁ etaṁ bhikkhuniṁ ‘evarūpā ca evarūpā ca sā bhaginī’ti, no ca kho attanā paṭicodessaṁ, na gaṇassa ārocessa”nti, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun knows that a nun has committed an offense entailing expulsion, but she neither confronts her herself nor tells the community, and afterward—whether that nun remains or has died or has been expelled or has converted—she says, ‘Venerables, although I previously knew that this nun was like this, I thought, “I will neither confront her myself nor tell the community,”’ she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on those who conceal offenses.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun knows that a nun has committed an offense entailing expulsion, but she neither confronts her herself nor tells a group of nuns, and afterward—whether that nun remains or has died or has been expelled or has converted—she says, ‘Venerables, although I previously knew that this nun was like this, I thought, I will neither confront her myself nor tell a group of nuns, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on those who conceal offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 7: siding with an ejected monk

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 7. Siding with One Ejected

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaraṁ appaṭikāraṁ akatasahāyaṁ tamanuvatteyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun takes sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends, the nuns should correct her like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun takes sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the rule, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends, the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: “eso kho, ayye, bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena ukkhitto, dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaro appaṭikāro akatasahāyo, māyye, etaṁ bhikkhuṁ anuvattī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerable, this monk has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. He’s disrespectful, hasn’t made amends, and hasn’t made friends. Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerable, this monk has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the rule, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. He’s disrespectful, hasn’t made amends, and hasn’t made friends. Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.’

segments.pali: evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

segments.pali: yāvatatiyaṁ ce samanubhāsiyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

segments.pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā ukkhittānuvattikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on taking sides with one who has been ejected.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she does not stop, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on taking sides with one who has been ejected.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Expulsion 8: behaving inappropriately in eight ways

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Expulsion 8. Eight Factors

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyya, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyya, santiṭṭheyya vā, sallapeyya vā, saṅketaṁ vā gaccheyya, purisassa vā abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyeyya, channaṁ vā anupaviseyya, kāyaṁ vā tadatthāya upasaṁhareyya etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā aṭṭhavatthukā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, a lustful nun consents to a lustful man holding her hand and the edge of her robe, and she stands with him and chats with him and goes to a rendezvous with him and consents to him coming to her and enters a covered place with him and disposes her body for him for that purpose, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule having eight parts.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, a lustful nun consents to a lustful man holding her hand and the edge of her robe, and she stands with him and chats with him and goes to an appointment with him and consents to him coming to her and enters a covered place with him and disposes her body for him for that purpose, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule having eight parts.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, aṭṭha pārājikā dhammā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, the eight rules on expulsion have been recited.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, the eight rules on expulsion have been recited.

segments.pali: Yesaṁ bhikkhunī aññataraṁ vā aññataraṁ vā āpajjitvā na labhati bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvāsaṁ

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun commits any one of them, she is no longer part of the community of nuns.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun commits any one of them, she is no longer part of the community of nuns.

segments.pali: yathā pure, tathā pacchā, pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: As before, so after: she’s expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: As before, so after: she’s expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The offenses entailing expulsion are finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The offenses entailing expulsion are finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The recitation of the rules of suspension

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Recitation of the Rules of Suspension

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo sattarasa saṅghādisesā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these seventeen rules on suspension come up for recitation.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, these seventeen rules on suspension come up for recitation.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 1: taking legal action

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 1: Litigious

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī ussayavādikā vihareyya gahapatinā vā gahapatiputtena vā dāsena vā kammakārena vā antamaso samaṇaparibbājakenāpi, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun takes legal action against a householder or a householder’s offspring or a slave or a worker or even toward a monastic or a wanderer, then that nun has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun takes legal action against a householder or a householder’s offspring or a slave or a worker or even toward a monastic or a wanderer, then that nun has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 2: ordaining a criminal

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 2: Ordaining a Criminal

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ coriṁ vajjhaṁ viditaṁ anapaloketvā rājānaṁ vā saṅghaṁ vā gaṇaṁ vā pūgaṁ vā seṇiṁ vā, aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun, without getting permission from the king or the Sangha or a community or an association or a society, knowingly gives the full admission to a female criminal who is known as sentenced to death, then, except when it is allowable, that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun, without getting permission from the king or the Sangha or a community or an association or a society, gives the full admission to a woman who she knows is a criminal and who is known as sentenced to death, then, except when it is allowable, that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 3: being alone

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 3. Walking Alone to the Next Village

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā rattiṁ vippavaseyya, ekā vā gaṇamhā ohiyeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself or spends the night apart by herself or lags behind her companions by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself or spends the night apart by herself or lags behind her companions by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 4: readmitting an ejected nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 4. Reinstating One Ejected

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ, anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāreyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun, without getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community, readmits a nun who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching and the Monastic Law and the Teacher’s instruction, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun, without getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the group, reinstates a nun who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the rule and the Monastic Law and the Teacher’s instruction, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 5: receiving food with lust

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 5: Receiving Food With Lust

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā, bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a lustful nun eats fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a lustful nun eats fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 6: encouraging a nun to receive food from a man with lust

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 6: Encouraging Receiving Food From One With Lust

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī evaṁ vadeyya: “kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā, iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā, taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun says, ‘Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,’ then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun says, ‘Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, Venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,’ then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 7: matchmaking

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 7. Matchmaking

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī sañcarittaṁ samāpajjeyya itthiyā vā purisamatiṁ, purisassa vā itthimatiṁ, jāyattane vā jārattane vā antamaso taṅkhaṇikāyapi, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun acts as a matchmaker, conveying a man’s intention to a woman or a woman’s intention to a man, for marriage or for an affair, even if just a brief one, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun acts as a matchmaker, conveying a man’s intention to a woman or a woman’s intention to a man, for marriage or for an affair, even if just a brief one, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 8: making a groundless accusation out of anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 8: Making a Groundless Accusation Out of Anger

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniṁ duṭṭhā dosā appatītā amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁseyya: “appeva nāma naṁ imamhā brahmacariyā cāveyya”nti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun who is angry and displeased groundlessly charges a nun with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make her leave the monastic life,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun who is angry and displeased groundlessly charges a nun with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make her leave the monastic life,

segments.pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamānā vā asa manuggāhīyamānā vā amūlakañceva taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ hoti, bhikkhunī ca dosaṁ patiṭṭhāti, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and then after some time, whether she is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is groundless, and she admits to her ill will, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and then after some time, whether she is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is groundless, and she admits to her ill will, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 9: making a groundless accusation out of anger, using a pretext

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 9: Making an Accusation Out of Anger on an Irrelevant Pretext

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniṁ duṭṭhā dosā appatītā aññabhāgiyassa adhikaraṇassa kiñcidesaṁ lesamattaṁ upādāya pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁseyya, “appeva nāma naṁ imamhā brahmacariyā cāveyya”nti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun who is angry and displeased, uses an unrelated legal issue as a pretext to charge a nun with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make her leave the monastic life,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun who is angry and displeased, uses an unrelated legal issue as a pretext to charge a nun with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make her leave the monastic life,

segments.pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamānā vā asamanuggāhīyamānā vā aññabhāgiyañceva taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ hoti. Kocideso lesamatto upādinno, bhikkhunī ca dosaṁ patiṭṭhāti, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and then after some time, whether she is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is unrelated and was used as a pretext, and she admits to her ill will, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and then after some time, whether she is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is unrelated and was used as pretext, and she admits to her ill will, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 10: renouncing the training in anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 10: Renouncing the Training

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun says in anger,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun says in anger,

segments.pali: “buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro, santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them,’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: “māyye kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca ‘buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro, santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti, abhiramāyye, svākkhāto dhammo, cara brahmacariyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerable, don’t say such things in anger, “I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.” Take delight, venerable; the Teaching is well proclaimed. Practice the spiritual life for the complete ending of suffering.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerable, don’t say such things in anger, I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.’ Take delight, Venerable; the Teaching is well proclaimed. Practice the spiritual life for the complete ending of suffering.’

segments.pali: evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

segments.pali: yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

segments.pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 11: accusing nuns of acting from bias

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 11: Getting Angry Over a Legal Issue

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun is angry because she has lost a legal case, saying,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun is angry because a legal issue has been decided against her, saying,

segments.pali: “chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear,’ then the nuns should correct her like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear,’  then the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: “māyye, kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca ‘chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo’ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerable, just because you’re angry that you’ve lost a legal case, don’t say, “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.”

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerable, just because you’re angry that a legal issue has been decided against you, don’t say, ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’

segments.pali: ayyā kho chandāpi gaccheyya, dosāpi gaccheyya, mohāpi gaccheyya, bhayāpi gaccheyyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Perhaps it’s you who are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Perhaps it’s you who are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’

english.author: pasanna

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 12: inappropriate socializing

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 12: Socializing

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo paneva saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If nuns socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If nuns socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct them like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then the nuns should correct them like this:

segments.pali: “bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Be secluded, Venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the Sisters.’

english.author: brahmali

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun says,

segments.pali: “saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha, mā tumhe nānā viharittha,

english.author: brahmali

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.

segments.pali: santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā, tā saṅgho na kiñci āha

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them.

segments.pali: tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha: ‘bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā, viviccathāyye, vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī”’ti,

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these thirty rules on relinquishment and confession come up for recitation.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, these thirty rules on relinquishment and confession come up for recitation.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on almsbowls

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 1: collecting almsbowls

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 1: Collecting Bowls

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī pattasannicayaṁ kareyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun collects almsbowls, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun collects almsbowls, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 2: distributing out-of-season robe-cloth

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 2: Sharing Out-of-Season Robe Cloth

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī akālacīvaraṁ “kālacīvara”nti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun determines out-of-season robe-cloth as ‘in-season’, and then distributes it, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun determines out-of-season robe-cloth as In-season’, and then distributes it, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 3: taking back a traded robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 3: Swapping Robes

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya: “handāyye, tuyhaṁ cīvaraṁ, āhara metaṁ cīvaraṁ, yaṁ tuyhaṁ tuyhamevetaṁ, yaṁ mayhaṁ mayhamevetaṁ, āhara metaṁ cīvaraṁ, sakaṁ paccāharā”ti acchindeyya vā acchindāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun trades robes with a nun and then says, ‘Here’s your robe; give me that robe of mine. That which is yours is yours, and that which is mine is mine. Give me that, and take back what’s yours;’ and she just takes it or has it taken, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun swaps robes with a nun and then says, ‘Here’s your robe; give me that robe of mine. That which is yours is yours, and that which is mine is mine. Give me that, and take back what’s yours;’ and she just takes it or has it taken, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 4: asking for something else

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 4: Asking for Something Else

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun asks for one thing and then for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun asks for one thing and then for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 5: exchanging for something else

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 5: Exchanging for Something Else

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali

english.text: Relinquishment and confession 11: getting an expensive heavy cloak in exchange

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 11: Heavy Cloaks

segments.pali: Garupāvuraṇaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā cetāpentiyā catukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpetabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun carries out an exchange to get a heavy cloak, it is to be worth at most four kaṁsa coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun carries out an exchange to get a heavy cloak, it is to be worth at most four _kaṁsa_ coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 12: getting an expensive light cloak in exchange

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 12: Light Cloaks

segments.pali: Lahupāvuraṇaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā cetāpentiyā aḍḍhateyyakaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpetabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun carries out an exchange to get a light cloak, it is to be worth at most two-and-a-half kaṁsa coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun carries out an exchange to get a light cloak, it is to be worth at most two-and-a-half _kaṁsa_ coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 13: keeping extra robe-cloth beyond ten days

english.author: pasanna

segments.pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhuniyā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine dasāhaparamaṁ atirekacīvaraṁ dhāretabbaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When her robe is finished and the robe season has ended, a nun should keep an extra robe for ten days at the most.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When her robe is finished and the robe season has ended, a nun should keep an extra robe for ten days at the most.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she keeps it longer than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she keeps it longer than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 14: staying apart from one’s robes

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 14: Staying Apart From Robes

segments.pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhuniyā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine ekarattampi ce bhikkhunī ticīvarena vippavaseyya, aññatra bhikkhunisammutiyā nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When her robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if a nun stays apart from her three robes even for a single day, except if the nuns have agreed, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When her robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if a nun stays apart from her three robes even for a single day, except if the nuns have agreed, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 15: keeping extra robe-cloth beyond a month

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 15: Out-of-Season Robe Cloth

segments.pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhuniyā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine bhikkhuniyā paneva akālacīvaraṁ uppajjeyya, ākaṅkhamānāya bhikkhuniyā paṭiggahetabbaṁ,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When her robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if out-of-season robe-cloth is offered to a nun, she may receive it if she wishes.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When her robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if out-of-season robe-cloth is given to a nun, she may receive it if she wishes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives it, she should quickly make a robe.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she receives it, she should quickly make a robe.

segments.pali: no cassa pāripūri, māsaparamaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā taṁ cīvaraṁ nikkhipitabbaṁ ūnassa pāripūriyā satiyā paccāsāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there is not enough cloth, but she is expecting more, she should keep it at most one month to make up the lack.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If there is not enough cloth, but she is expecting more, she should keep it at most one month to make up the lack.

segments.pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyya satiyāpi paccāsāya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she keeps it longer than that, then even if she expects more cloth, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she keeps it longer than that, then even if she expects more cloth, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 16: asking an unrelated person for a robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 16: Asking an Unrelated Person for Robes

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpeyya aññatra samayā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun asks an unrelated male or female householder for a robe, except on an appropriate occasion, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun asks an unrelated male or female householder for a robe, except on an appropriate occasion, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo acchinnacīvarā vā hoti bhikkhunī, naṭṭhacīvarā vā, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: her robes are stolen or her robes are lost.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: her robes are stolen or her robes are lost.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 17: accepting too many robes

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 17: Accepting Too Many Robes

segments.pali: Tañce aññātako gahapati vā gahapatānī vā bahūhi cīvarehi abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyya, santaruttaraparamaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā tato cīvaraṁ sāditabbaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If an unrelated male or female householder invites that nun to take many robes, she should accept at most one sarong and one upper robe.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If an unrelated male or female householder invites that nun to take many robes, she should accept at most one sarong and one upper robe.

segments.pali: Tato ce uttari sādiyeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she accepts more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she accepts more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.text: If two male or female householders have set aside separate robe funds for an unrelated nun, thinking,

english.text: ‘With these separate robe funds we’ll buy separate robe-cloths and give them to nun so-and-so;’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and if that nun, without first being invited, goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth she wants, saying,

english.text: and if that nun, without first being invited, goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth she wants, saying,

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniṁ paneva uddissa rājā vā rājabhoggo vā brāhmaṇo vā gahapatiko vā dūtena cīvaracetāpannaṁ pahiṇeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a king, a king’s employee, a brahmin, or a householder sends a robe fund for a nun by messenger, saying,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a king, a king’s employee, a brahmin, or a householder sends a robe fund for a nun by messenger, saying,

segments.pali: “iminā cīvaracetāpannena cīvaraṁ cetāpetvā itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ cīvarena acchādehī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Buy robe-cloth with this robe fund and give it to nun so-and-so,’

english.text: ‘Buy robe-cloth with this robe fund and give it to nun so-and-so,’

segments.pali: So ce dūto taṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the messenger goes to that nun and says,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and the messenger goes to that nun and says,

segments.pali: “idaṁ kho, ayye, ayyaṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannaṁ ābhataṁ, paṭiggaṇhātāyyā cīvaracetāpanna”nti.

english.text: ‘Venerable, I have brought a robe fund for you. Please receive it,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerable, I have brought a robe fund for you. Please receive it,’

segments.pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā so dūto evamassa vacanīyo:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then that nun should reply,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then that nun should reply,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘We don’t receive robe funds, but we do receive allowable robe-cloth at the right time.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘We don’t receive robe funds, but we do receive allowable robe-cloth at the right time.’

segments.pali: So ce dūto taṁ bhikkhuniṁ evaṁ vadeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that messenger says,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that messenger says,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Is there anyone who provides services for you?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Is there anyone who provides services for you?’

segments.pali: cīvaratthikāya, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyā veyyāvaccakaro niddisitabbo ārāmiko vā upāsako vā:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun, if she needs robe-cloth, should point out a monastery worker or a lay follower and say,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: the nun, if she needs robe-cloth, should point out a monastery worker or a lay follower and say,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘They provide services for the nuns.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘That person provides services for the nuns.’

segments.pali: So ce dūto taṁ veyyāvaccakaraṁ saññāpetvā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the messenger instructs that service provider and then returns to the nun and says,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If the messenger instructs that service-provider and then returns to the nun and says,

segments.pali: “yaṁ kho, ayye, ayyā veyyāvaccakaraṁ niddisi, saññatto so mayā, upasaṅkamatāyyā kālena, cīvarena taṁ acchādessatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerable, I have instructed the service provider you pointed out. Please go to them at the right time and they’ll give you robe-cloth,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerable, I have instructed the service-provider you pointed out. Please go to them at the right time and he’ll give you robe-cloth,’

segments.pali: Cīvaratthikāya, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyā veyyāvaccakaro upasaṅkamitvā dvattikkhattuṁ codetabbo sāretabbo:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then, if that nun needs robe-cloth, she should go to that service provider and prompt them and remind them two or three times, saying,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then, if that nun needs robe-cloth, she should go to that service-provider and prompt and remind them two or three times, saying,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘I need robe-cloth.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: “I need robe-cloth.’

english.text: If she then gets robe-cloth, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she then gets robe-cloth, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she does not get it, she should stand in silence for it at most six times.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then gets robe-cloth, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she then gets robe-cloth, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she makes any further effort and then gets robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.text: If she does not get robe-cloth, she should go to the owners of that robe fund, or send a message, saying,

english.author: pasanna

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 24: An Almsbowl With Fewer Than Five Mends

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī ūnapañcabandhanena pattena aññaṁ navaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun exchanges an almsbowl with fewer than five mends for a new almsbowl, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun exchanges an almsbowl with fewer than five mends for a new almsbowl, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā so patto bhikkhuniparisāya nissajjitabbo,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun should relinquish that almsbowl to a gathering of nuns.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: That nun should relinquish that almsbowl to a gathering of nuns.

segments.pali: yo ca tassā bhikkhuniparisāya pattapariyanto, so tassā bhikkhuniyā padātabbo:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She should then be given the last almsbowl belonging to that gathering:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: She should then be given the last almsbowl belonging to that gathering:

segments.pali: “ayaṁ te bhikkhuni patto yāvabhedanāya dhāretabbo”ti, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Nun, this bowl is yours. Keep it until it breaks.’ This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Nun, this bowl is yours. Keep it until it breaks.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 25: storing the five tonics too long

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 25: The Five Tonics

segments.pali: Yāni kho pana tāni gilānānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ paṭisāyanīyāni bhesajjāni, seyyathidaṁ: sappi navanītaṁ telaṁ madhu phāṇitaṁ, tāni paṭiggahetvā sattāhaparamaṁ sannidhikārakaṁ paribhuñjitabbāni.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After being received, the tonics allowable for sick nuns—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, and syrup—should be used from storage for at most seven days.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: After being received, the tonics allowable for sick monks—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, and syrup: should be used from storage for at most seven days.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If one uses them longer than that, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she keeps it longer than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 26: taking back robe-cloth in anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 26: Taking Back Robe-Cloth

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā acchindeyya vā acchindāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun herself gives a robe to a nun, but then, in anger, takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun herself gives a robe to a nun, but then, in anger, takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun herself asks for thread, and then has weavers weave her robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun herself asks for thread, and then has weavers weave her robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 28: Bribing Weavers

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniṁ paneva uddissa aññātako gahapati vā gahapatānī vā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpeyya, tatra cesā bhikkhunī pubbe appavāritā tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a male or female householder is having robe-cloth woven by weavers for an unrelated nun and, without first being invited, that nun goes to those weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth she wants, saying,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a male or female householder is having robe-cloth woven by weavers for an unrelated nun and, without first being invited, that nun goes to those weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth she wants, saying,

segments.pali: “idaṁ kho āvuso cīvaraṁ maṁ uddissa viyyati, āyatañca karotha, vitthatañca appitañca suvītañca suppavāyitañca suvilekhitañca suvitacchitañca karotha,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘This robe-cloth that you are weaving for me, make it long and wide; make it closely woven, well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘This robe-cloth that you are weaving for me, make it long and wide; make it closely woven, well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed,

segments.pali: appeva nāma mayampi āyasmantānaṁ kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyyāmā”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and perhaps I will even give you a small gift,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and perhaps I will even give you a small gift,’

segments.pali: evañca sā bhikkhunī vatvā kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyya antamaso piṇḍapātamattampi, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then, in saying that and afterwards giving them a small gift, even a bit of almsfood, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then, in saying that and afterwards giving them a small gift, even a bit of almsfood, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 29: storing haste-cloth too long

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 29: Haste-Cloth

segments.pali: Dasāhānāgataṁ kattikatemāsikapuṇṇamaṁ bhikkhuniyā paneva accekacīvaraṁ uppajjeyya, accekaṁ maññamānāya bhikkhuniyā paṭiggahetabbaṁ, paṭiggahetvā yāva cīvarakālasamayaṁ nikkhipitabbaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When there are ten days left to the Kattika full moon that ends the first rainy-season residence and a haste-cloth is given to a nun, she may receive it if she regards it as urgent. She may then store it until the end of the robe season.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When there are ten days left to the Kattika full moon that ends the first rainy-season residence and haste-cloth is given to a nun, she may receive it if she regards it as urgent. She may then store it until the end of the robe season.

segments.pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stores it beyond that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she stores it beyond that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun diverts to herself material support that she knows was intended for the Sangha, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna

english.text: If, when a monk is eating, a nun attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī āmakadhaññaṁ viññatvā vā viññāpetvā vā bhajjitvā vā bhajjāpetvā vā koṭṭetvā vā koṭṭāpetvā vā pacitvā vā pacāpetvā vā bhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun asks for or has someone else ask for raw grain, or she roasts it or has it roasted, or she pounds it or has it pounded, or she cooks it or has it cooked, and she then eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun asks for or has someone else ask for raw grain, or she roasts it or has it roasted, or she pounds it or has it pounded, or she cooks it or has it cooked, and she then eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun disposes of feces or urine or trash or food scraps over a wall or over an encircling wall, or she has it disposed of in this way, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun disposes of feces or urine or trash or food scraps over a wall or over an encircling wall, or she has it disposed of in this way, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun is having a bathing robe made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā vihareyya gahapatinā vā gahapatiputtena vā, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun is socializing with a householder or a householder’s offspring, the nuns should correct her like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun is socializing with a householder or a householder’s offspring, the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: “māyye, saṁsaṭṭhā vihari gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenāpi, viviccāyye, vivekaññeva bhaginiyā saṅgho vaṇṇetī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring. Be secluded, venerable. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring. Be secluded, Venerable. The Sangha praises seclusion for the Sisters.’

segments.pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya,

english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: she is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes; she is traveling; she is on a boat; it is a big occasion; it is a meal given by a monastic.

english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: she is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes; she is traveling; she is on a boat; it is a big occasion; it is a meal given by a monastic.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun goes to a family and is invited to take cookies or crackers, she may accept two or three bowlfuls if she wishes.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun goes to a family and is invited to take pastries or baked goods, she may accept two or three bowlfuls if she wishes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she accepts more than that, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she accepts more than that, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dvattipattapūre paṭiggahetvā tato nīharitvā bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvibhajitabbaṁ, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she accepts two or three bowlfuls, she should take it away and share it with the nuns. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she accepts two or three bowlfuls, she should take it away and share it with the nuns. This is the proper procedure.

english.text: If a nun says to a nun, ‘Come, let’s go to the village or town for alms,’ and then, whether she has had food given to her or not, sends her away, saying, ‘Go away, I’m not comfortable talking or sitting with you, but only if I talk and sit by myself,’ and she does so only for this reason and no other, she commits an offense entailing confession.
