english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 130: Staying with an Army

segments.pali: Siyā ca tassā bhikkhuniyā kocideva paccayo senaṁ gamanāya, dirattatirattaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā senāya vasitabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari vaseyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun has a reason for going to the army, she may stay with the army for two or three nights. If she stays longer than that, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that nun has a reason for going to the army, she may stay with the army for two or three nights.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 131: seeing an army in action

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 131. Viewing a Military Action

segments.pali: Dirattatirattaṁ ce bhikkhunī senāya vasamānā uyyodhikaṁ vā balaggaṁ vā senābyūhaṁ vā anīkadassanaṁ vā gaccheyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun who is staying with an army for two or three nights goes to a battle, a troop review, a massing of the army, or a troop inspection, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun who is staying with an army for two or three nights goes to a battle, a troop review, a massing of the army, or a troop inspection, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 132: drinking alcohol

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 132: Drinking Alcohol

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun drinks this or that kind of alcoholic drink, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun drinks this or that kind of alcoholic drink, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 133: tickling

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 133: Tickling

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun tickles someone, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun tickles someone, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 134: playing in water

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 134: Playing

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun plays in water, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun plays in water, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 135: disrespect

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 135: Disrespect

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun is disrespectful, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun is disrespectful, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 136: scaring a nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 136: Scaring

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniṁ bhiṁsāpeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun scares a nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun scares a nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The thirteenth subchapter on visiting is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The thirteenth sub-chapter on visiting is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on fire

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 137: lighting a fire

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 137: Lighting a Fire

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī agilānā visibbanāpekkhā jotiṁ samādaheyya vā samādahāpeyya vā aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun who is not sick lights a fire to warm herself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun who is not sick lights a fire to warm herself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 138: bathing too often

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 138: Bathing

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyeyya aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, except on an appropriate occasion, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, except on an appropriate occasion, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo “diyaḍḍho māso seso gimhāna”nti “vassānassa paṭhamo māso” iccete aḍḍhateyyamāsā uṇhasamayo, pariḷāhasamayo, gilānasamayo, kammasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo, vātavuṭṭhisamayo, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of summer and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season; she is sick; she is working; she is traveling; there is wind and rain.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of the hot season and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season; she is sick; she is working; she is traveling; there is wind and rain.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 139: not making stains on a new robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 139: Making Stains

segments.pali: Navaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā cīvaralābhāya tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ ādātabbaṁ nīlaṁ vā kaddamaṁ vā kāḷasāmaṁ vā. Anādā ce bhikkhunī tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ navaṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When a nun gets a new robe, she should apply one of three kinds of stains: blue-green, mud color, or dark brown. If a nun uses a new robe without applying any of the three kinds of stains, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When a nun gets a new robe, she should apply one of three kinds of stains: blue-green, mud-color, or dark brown. If a nun uses a new robe without applying any of the three kinds of stains, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 140: using assigned property that has not been relinquished

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 140: Transferring to Another

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā sikkhamānāya vā sāmaṇerassa vā sāmaṇeriyā vā sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappetvā apaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun herself assigns the ownership of a robe to a monk, a nun, a trainee nun, a novice monk, or a novice nun, and she then uses it without the other first relinquishing it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun herself assigns the ownership of a robe to a monk, to a nun, to a trainee nun, to a novice monk, or to a novice nun, and she then uses it without the other first relinquishing it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 141: hiding a robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 141: Hiding Robes

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā nisīdanaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ vā kāyabandhanaṁ vā apanidheyya vā apanidhāpeyya vā antamaso hasāpekkhāpi, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun hides a nun’s bowl, robe, sitting mat, needle case, or belt, or she has it hidden, even just for a laugh, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun hides a nun’s bowl, robe, sitting mat, needle case, or belt, or she has it hidden, even just for a laugh, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 142: intentionally killing a living being

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 142: Intentionally Killing Living Creatures

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun intentionally kills a living being, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun intentionally kills a living being, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 143: using water with living beings in it

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 143: Using Water With Living Creatures

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun uses water that she knows contains living beings, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun uses water that she knows contains living beings, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 144: reopening a settled legal issue

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 144: Reopening a Legal Issue

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ yathādhammaṁ nihatādhikaraṇaṁ punakammāya ukkoṭeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun reopens a legal issue that she knows has been legitimately settled, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun reopens a legal issue that she knows has been legitimately settled, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 145: traveling with thieves

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 145: Travelling With Thieves

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ theyyasatthena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya antamaso gāmantarampi, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun knowingly travels by arrangement with a group of thieves, even just to the next inhabited area, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun knowingly travels by appointment with a group of thieves, even just to the next village, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 146: not giving up a bad view

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 146: Obstructions

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun says,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun says,

segments.pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstructive are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: “māyye evaṁ avaca, mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi, na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ, na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘No, venerable, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘No, Venerable, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

segments.pali: anekapariyāyenāyye antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā, alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstructive things to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

segments.pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her give up that view.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then gives it up, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourteenth subchapter on fire is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The fourteenth sub-chapter on fire is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on view

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 147: living with an ejected nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 147: Living With an Ejected Monastic

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ tathāvādiniyā bhikkhuniyā akaṭānudhammāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhāya saddhiṁ sambhuñjeyya vā, saṁvaseyya vā, saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun lives, does formal meetings, or shares a sleeping place with a nun who she knows is saying such things, who has not made amends according to the rule, and who has not given up that view, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun lives, does formal meetings, or shares a sleeping place with a nun who she knows is saying such things, who has not made amends according to the rule, and who has not given up that view, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 148: supporting a novice nun with a bad view

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 148: Obstructions and a Novice

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Also if a novice nun says,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Also if a novice monastic says,

segments.pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstructive are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: “māyye, samaṇuddese evaṁ avaca, mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi, na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ, na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘No, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘No, Venerable, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

segments.pali: anekapariyāyenāyye, samaṇuddese antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā, alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstructive things to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

segments.pali: Evañca sā samaṇuddesā bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā samaṇuddesā bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that novice nun continues as before, she should be told:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that novice monastic continues as before, she should be told:

segments.pali: “ajjatagge te, ayye, samaṇuddese na ceva so bhagavā satthā apadisitabbo,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘From today on you may not refer to the Buddha as your teacher.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘From today on you may not refer to the Buddha as your teacher.

segments.pali: yampi caññā samaṇuddesā labhanti bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ dirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ, sāpi te natthi,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And, unlike other novices, you can no longer share a sleeping place with the nuns for two or three nights.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: And you can no longer share a sleeping place with the monks for two or three nights, as can other novice monastics.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Go! Away with you!’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Go! Away with you!’

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ tathānāsitaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ upalāpeyya vā, upaṭṭhāpeyya vā, sambhuñjeyya vā, saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun befriends that novice nun, or she is attended on by her, lives with her, or shares a sleeping place with her, even though she knows that she has been expelled in this way, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun supports that novice monastic, or she is attended on by her, lives with her, or shares a sleeping place with her, even though she knows that she has been expelled in this way, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 149: not accepting legitimate correction

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 149: Legitimate Correction

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamānā evaṁ vadeyya: “na tāvāhaṁ, ayye, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi, yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuniṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī”ti, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun, when legitimately corrected by the nuns, says, ‘I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a nun who is an expert on the Monastic Law,’ she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun, when legitimately corrected by the monks, says, I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a nun who is an expert on the Monastic Law,’ she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Sikkhamānāya, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyā aññātabbaṁ paripucchitabbaṁ paripañhitabbaṁ, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A nun who is training should understand, should question, should enquire. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A nun who is training should understand, should question, should enquire. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 150: disparaging the training rules

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 150: Annoyance

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadeyya: “kiṁ panimehi khuddānukhuddakehi sikkhāpadehi uddiṭṭhehi, yāvadeva kukkuccāya vihesāya vilekhāya saṁvattantī”ti, sikkhāpadavivaṇṇake pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the Monastic Code is being recited, if a nun says, ‘What’s the point of reciting these minor training rules, when they just lead to anxiety, oppression, and annoyance?’ then in disparaging the training rules, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When the Monastic Code is being recited, if a nun says, ‘What’s the point of reciting these minor and insignificant training rules, when they just lead to anxiety, oppression, and annoyance?’ then in disparaging the training rules, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 151: deception

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 151: Deception

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī anvaddhamāsaṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadeyya:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: During the half-monthly recitation of the Monastic Code, a nun might say,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: During the half-monthly recitation of the Monastic Code, a nun might say,

segments.pali: “idāneva kho ahaṁ, ayye, jānāmi ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Just now did I find out that this rule too has come down in the Monastic Code, is included in the Monastic Code, and comes up for recitation every half-month.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Just now did I find out that this rule too has come down in the Monastic Code, is included in the Monastic Code, and comes up for recitation every half-month.’

segments.pali: tañce bhikkhuniṁ aññā bhikkhuniyo jāneyyuṁ nisinnapubbaṁ imāya bhikkhuniyā dvattikkhattuṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne, ko pana vādo bhiyyo, na ca tassā bhikkhuniyā aññāṇakena mutti atthi, yañca tattha āpattiṁ āpannā, tañca yathādhammo kāretabbo, uttari cassā moho āropetabbo:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If other nuns know that that nun has previously sat through at least two or three recitations of the Monastic Code, then that nun is not let off because of ignorance, and she is to be dealt with according to the rule. Further, she should be charged with deception:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If other nuns know that that nun has previously sat through at least two or three recitations of the Monastic Code, then that nun is not let off because of ignorance, and she is to be dealt with according to the rule. Further, she should be charged with deception:

segments.pali: “tassā te, ayye, alābhā, tassā te dulladdhaṁ, yaṁ tvaṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne na sādhukaṁ aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi karosī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘It’s a loss for you that you don’t pay proper attention during the recitation of the Monastic Code.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: It’s a loss for you that you don’t pay proper attention during the recitation of the Monastic Code.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And for the act of deception, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: And for the act of deception, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 152: hitting in anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 152: Hitting

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā kupitā anattamanā pahāraṁ dadeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun hits a nun in anger, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun hits a nun in anger, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 153: raising a hand in anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 153: Raising a Hand

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā kupitā anattamanā talasattikaṁ uggireyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun raises a hand in anger against a nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun raises a hand in anger against a nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 154: making a groundless accusation

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 154: Groundless Accusation

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniṁ amūlakena saṅghādisesena anuddhaṁseyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun groundlessly charges a nun with an offense entailing suspension, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun groundlessly charges a nun with an offense entailing suspension, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 155: intentionally making a nun anxious

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 155: Intentionally Making a Monastic Anxious

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadaheyya, “itissā muhuttampi aphāsu bhavissatī”ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun intentionally makes a nun anxious, thinking, ‘In this way she will be ill at ease at least for a moment,’ and she does so only for this reason and no other, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun intentionally makes a nun anxious, thinking, In this way she will be ill at ease at least for a moment,’ and she does so only for this reason and no other, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 156: eavesdropping

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 156: Eavesdropping

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ upassutiṁ tiṭṭheyya, “yaṁ imā bhaṇissanti, taṁ sossāmī”ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun eavesdrops on nuns who are arguing and disputing, thinking, ‘I’ll hear what they say,’ and she does so only for this reason and no other, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun eavesdrops on nuns who are arguing and disputing, thinking, ‘I’ll hear what they say,’ and she does so only for this reason and no other, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifteenth subchapter on view is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The fifteenth sub-chapter on one who has been ejected is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on legitimate correction

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 157: criticizing a legitimate legal procedure

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 157: Obstructing a Legal Procedure

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī dhammikānaṁ kammānaṁ chandaṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun gives her consent to legitimate legal procedures, and then criticizes them afterwards, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun gives her consent to legitimate legal procedures, and then criticizes them afterwards, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 158: leaving without giving consent

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 158: Leaving Without Giving Consent

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkameyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion, if a nun gets up from her seat and leaves without first giving her consent, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion, if a nun gets up from her seat and leaves without first giving her consent, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 159: criticizing a robe distribution

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 159: Making Stains

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena cīvaraṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyya, “yathāsanthutaṁ bhikkhuniyo saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇāmentī”ti, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun gives out a robe as part of a unanimous Sangha and then criticizes it afterwards, saying, ‘The nuns are diverting the Sangha’s material support according to friendship,’ she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun gives out a robe as part of a unanimous Sangha and then criticizes it afterwards, saying, ‘The nuns are diverting the Sangha’s material gains according to friendship,’ she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 160: diverting gains intended for the Sangha

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 160: Diverting Gains

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ puggalassa pariṇāmeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun diverts to an individual material support that she knows was intended for the Sangha, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun diverts to an individual material support that she knows was intended for the Sangha, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 161: picking up precious things

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 161: Precious Things

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā aññatra ajjhārāmā vā ajjhāvasathā vā uggaṇheyya vā uggaṇhāpeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or she has it picked up, except within a monastery or inside a lodging, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or she has it picked up, except within a monastery or inside a lodging, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Ratanaṁ vā pana bhikkhuniyā ratanasammataṁ vā ajjhārāme vā ajjhāvasathe vā uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipitabbaṁ, “yassa bhavissati, so harissatī”ti, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or she has it picked up, within a monastery or inside a lodging, she should put it aside with the thought, ‘Whoever owns it will come and get it.’ This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If she picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or she has it picked up, within a monastery or inside a lodging, she should put it aside with the thought, ‘Whoever owns it will fetch it.’ This is the proper procedure.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 162: making an inappropriate needle case

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 162: Needle Case

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī aṭṭhimayaṁ vā dantamayaṁ vā visāṇamayaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ kārāpeyya, bhedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun has a needle case made from bone, ivory, or horn, it is to be destroyed, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun has a needle case made from bone, ivory, or horn, it is to be destroyed, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 163: making an inappropriate bed or bench

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 163: Beds and Benches

segments.pali: Navaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā kārayamānāya aṭṭhaṅgulapādakaṁ kāretabbaṁ sugataṅgulena aññatra heṭṭhimāya aṭaniyā. Taṁ atikkāmentiyā chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun is having a new bed or bench made, it is to have legs eight standard fingerbreadths long below the lowest frame. If the legs exceed that, they are to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun is having a new bed or bench made, it is to have legs eight standard fingerbreadths long below the lowest frame. If the legs exceed that, they are to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 164: making an upholstered bed or bench

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 164: Upholstery

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā tūlonaddhaṁ kārāpeyya, uddālanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun has a bed or a bench made upholstered with cotton down, it is to be stripped, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun has a bed or a bench made upholstered with cotton down, it is to be stripped, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 165: making an oversize itch cloth

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 165: Itch Bandage

segments.pali: Kaṇḍuppaṭicchādiṁ pana bhikkhuniyā kārayamānāya pamāṇikā kāretabbā, tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ, dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā, tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo. Taṁ atikkāmentiyā chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun is having an itch-covering cloth made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun is having an itch-covering cloth made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 166: making an oversize robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 166: Robe Size

segments.pali: Yā pana bhikkhunī sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ cīvaraṁ kārāpeyya, atirekaṁ vā, chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun has a robe made that is the standard robe size or larger, it is to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a nun has a robe made that is the standard robe measure or larger, it is to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatridaṁ sugatassa sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ, dīghaso nava vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā, tiriyaṁ cha vidatthiyo, idaṁ sugatassa sugatacīvarappamāṇanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the standard robe size: nine standard handspans long and six wide.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: This is the standard robe measure: nine standard handspans long and six wide.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixteenth subchapter on legitimate correction is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The sixteenth sub-chapter on obstructing a legal procedure is finished

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, chasaṭṭhisatā pācittiyā dhammā.

english.text: Venerables, the one hundred and sixty-six rules on confession have been recited.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, the hundred and sixty-six rules on confession have been recited.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna

english.author: pasanna

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’

english.author: brahmali

english.author: pasanna
